translator: Swami Adidevananda (per Sri Ramakrishna Math edition; bhagavad-gita.us reproduces this English rendering of Ramanuja's Gita-bhasya)
verbatim_excerpt: 2.6 – 2.8 If you say, ‘After beginning the war, if we withdraw from the battle, the sons of Dhrtarastra will slay us all forcibly’, be it so. I think that even to be killed by them, who do not know the difference between righteousness and unrighteousness, is better for us than gaining unrighteous vi
copyright_holder: Sri Ramakrishna Math, Chennai (publisher of Swami Adidevananda's English translation of Ramanuja's Gita-bhasya). Site bhagavad-gita.us reproduces the translation; we cite a short fair-use excerpt only.
notes: Full commentary length 500 chars. Ramanuja gives a single combined gloss for verses 2.6-2.8; the captured excerpt is a literal fair-use prefix of that combined commentary and applies to every verse in the cluster. Source uses non-IAST transliteration (e.g. 'Krsna', 'Bhisma', 'Drona') and curly quotes; preserved verbatim.
